prompt:
Ticket: collector for the asset-inventory service.

We need `pagehaul.ps1` — a script the audit cron runs to drain the inventory API and emit one summary line. The API is cursor-paginated:

    GET <base>/assets?limit=N            first page
    GET <base>/assets?cursor=T&limit=N   every later page
    -> {"items": [{"id": "a-101", "site": "web"}, ...], "next": "t0180" | null}

Rules of the road, straight from the vendor doc: cursors are opaque strings — send back exactly what you were given, never build one yourself. The FIRST request must not send a cursor parameter at all. EVERY request must carry `limit=<your page size>`; the server 400s without it. A full page always carries a `next` token even when it happens to be the last of the data, so sooner or later you fetch a page whose `items` is empty and whose `next` is null — that's the stop signal (a partial page with next=null stops too, obviously). Do not fetch anything after next comes back null.

Usage: `pwsh -NoProfile -File ./pagehaul.ps1 -BaseUrl http://127.0.0.1:8080 -PageSize 5`

Output: exactly one line on stdout, compact JSON (ConvertTo-Json -Compress shape), keys in this order:

    {"total":15,"pages":4,"sites":[{"site":"Web","count":2},...],"ids":["a-101",...]}

where total = item count, pages = number of GETs you made, sites = per-site counts sorted by site name with the ORDINAL comparer (site tags are messy in this system — some hosts were registered as `Web` next to `web` — and ops wants to see the miscased ones as their own rows, uppercase-first, NOT merged or culture-sorted), and ids = every asset id in the order the API delivered them. Empty inventory must still print the line with zeros and empty arrays.

Acceptance is `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_pagehaul.ps1` — it runs a local mock of the vendor API and also checks the exact request sequence you sent, so no extra or missing calls. Don't touch the test file or the mock.
notes: 6P6 item 1: cursor-paginated collector. Python stdlib mock server (per wave-6 6P6 law) started/stopped by the harness via Start-Process + atomic port file; request log pinned server-side so page-size handling, opaque-cursor passthrough, the no-cursor first call, the empty-page terminal fetch (full last page => next token => one more empty fetch) and the no-extra-calls rule all have teeth. Ordinal aggregation pinned via a 'Web' site tag sorting before 'cache' (linguistic sort would interleave). Distinct from go-pagewalk (pagination FORMAT breadth: Link headers + body cursors + dedupe) and py-cursorfeed (server-side cursor lifecycle with expiry): this is single-format cursor exhaustion + pinned request accounting in pwsh. catalog wave6/6P6/1.
